rules.statement: A source file containing structural markers of legacy / shim / deprecation patterns MUST be flagged as a candidate for evolutionary-purity review. This rule is a low-cost pre-selector for modernization.legacy_scars and does not itself produce a verdict.
rules.rationale: Per ADR-043 D7: LOC-style fan-out across 872 files at ~17s per LLM call exceeds the 600s sensor SLA. A regex pre-selector reduces the LLM verdict scope to files that contain at least one structural marker (legacy import, shim comment, deprecated parameter name).

rules.statement: Source code SHOULD be free of obsolete structural shims, unused parameters from prior iterations, and internal logic wrappers that bypass the Universal Workflow Pattern.
rules.rationale: To visualize the gap between the Historical Body and the Future Truth defined in REBIRTH v0.1.

rules.statement: A public symbol that self-declares as deprecated (a '.. deprecated::' directive, a DeprecationWarning in its body, or a first-line docstring self-declaration, including module-level attribution) AND has zero inbound call edges in the knowledge graph outside tests/ MUST be flagged as a dead shim. Properties are excluded (attribute reads are not call edges); the published __all__ extension contract and runtime-dispatch surfaces are graced (their callers are invisible to the static graph). Resolution is governed verify-then-delete: confirm no dynamic or string-keyed dispatch or config reference before removal.
rules.rationale: ADR-151 (#807): the legacy ladder (legacy_signal reporting, legacy_scars advisory) never escalates - six self-declared dead shims survived up to seven months with zero callers until an external review hand-found them (#804). The conjunction is the precision: 2,012 symbols have zero inbound edges (mostly legitimate entry points) and prose legacy-mentions intersect that set 60 times, while the measured self-declaration-AND-orphan join fires on exactly one true positive on the day-one tree. Static-graph ceiling per #801: zero inbound edges is necessary but not sufficient evidence for deletion - ADR-151 D4's dynamic-dispatch re-verification is the compensating control. Detection: DeadShimSensor (graph-backed, ADR-091 sensor model); enforcement starts at reporting, promotion gated on measured false-positive rate including the non-enumerated dispatch surfaces (ADR-151 D3).
